datasets: 1,001 annotated real diagrams from Krishnamurthy et al. (2016)
claims: LVLMs can accurately identify and reason about entities in diagrams, whether represented textually or visually.
LVLMs struggle with identifying and reasoning about relations in synthetic diagrams.
LVLMs perform significantly better on relation recognition in real diagrams compared to synthetic ones, but this is largely due to reliance on background knowledge.
Models leverage pre-existing knowledge as a shortcut to answer relation questions, even hallucinating relations when visual evidence is absent or contradictory.
Impressive performance on diagram reasoning benchmarks is an illusion stemming from confounding factors such as model-internal knowledge rather than genuine diagram understanding.
limitations: Evaluation does not cover all types of diagrams due to practical constraints.
Findings may not generalize to every Vision-Language Model or diagram domain.
Synthetic diagrams lack semantic context and real-world complexity, limiting ecological validity.
Reliance on multiple-choice question answering may constrain model responses and limit depth of reasoning assessment.
